requirements: The core contributions of the paper 'ContiguousKV: Accelerating LLM Prefill with Granularity-Aligned KV Cache Management' have been reproduced.

sub_tasks.requirements: Models, datasets, and the base inference framework required by the paper are available.
Code can load and run inference with the Qwen2.5-7B model.

verification.spec: Load Qwen2.5-7B via the implementation's model loader and run a forward pass on a short prompt (e.g., 'Hello, world'); assert the model returns logits with shape [batch, seq_len, vocab_size] and produces a non-empty generated token. Verify model config matches Qwen2.5-7B (hidden_size=3584, num_hidden_layers=28).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code can load and run inference with the Qwen2.5-14B model.

verification.spec: Load Qwen2.5-14B via the implementation's model loader and run a forward pass on a short prompt; assert the model returns logits with valid shape and produces a generated token. Verify model config matches Qwen2.5-14B (num_hidden_layers=48).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code can load and run inference with the Qwen2.5-32B model.

verification.spec: Load Qwen2.5-32B via the implementation's model loader (using offloading if needed within 80GB GPU memory budget) and run a forward pass on a short prompt; assert the model returns logits with valid shape and generates a token. Verify model config matches Qwen2.5-32B (num_hidden_layers=64).

sub_tasks.requirements: The SST-2 dataset for sentiment analysis has been prepared for evaluation.

verification.spec: Verify that the SST-2 dataset (sentiment analysis, GLUE) has been downloaded and is accessible (e.g., via Hugging Face datasets load_dataset('glue','sst2') or a local file) and contains the expected train/validation splits with sentence/label fields.

sub_tasks.requirements: The Subj dataset for subjectivity classification has been prepared for evaluation.

verification.spec: Verify that the Subj dataset (subjectivity classification, Pang & Lee 2004) has been downloaded and is accessible as a labeled corpus with subjective/objective classes loadable by the evaluation harness.

sub_tasks.requirements: The TREC dataset for question classification has been prepared for evaluation.

verification.spec: Verify that the TREC question classification dataset is downloaded and accessible (e.g., via Hugging Face datasets load_dataset('trec')) with question text and coarse label fields.

sub_tasks.requirements: The RTE dataset for textual entailment has been prepared for evaluation.

verification.spec: Verify that the RTE dataset (textual entailment, GLUE) is downloaded and accessible (e.g., via load_dataset('glue','rte')) with premise/hypothesis/label fields.

sub_tasks.requirements: The FlexGen LLM offloading inference framework is available and serves as the base implementation that has been modified to support Qwen2.5 series models.

verification.spec: Verify that the codebase contains a FlexGen-derived base (e.g., flexgen module/package or equivalent imports in InferenceEngine) and that classes/functions for managing OffloadingPolicy or per-layer KV offloading exist. Use importlib to import the inference engine module and assert references to FlexGen's data structures (e.g., TorchDevice, TorchDisk, or analogous) are present.

sub_tasks.requirements: A multi-tier storage hierarchy (GPU HBM, CPU DRAM, NVMe SSD) is set up so that prefix KV cache can be persistently stored on the SSD and tiered through CPU and GPU memory.

verification.spec: Verify the storage hierarchy by (1) querying CUDA device for available GPU HBM, (2) confirming CPU DRAM region usable for cache, (3) confirming an NVMe SSD mount point holds prefix KV files. Run a small script that writes a tensor to SSD, mmaps/loads it into CPU pinned memory, and transfers to GPU, confirming the three-tier path works end-to-end.

sub_tasks.requirements: Shared prefix KV cache and Re-Prefill workload configurations have been correctly constructed.

Shared prefixes are constructed by sampling few-shot examples from each category of the training dataset following a normal distribution, using the same prompt template as Gao et al. (LM-BFF) and ReFusion.

verification.spec: Inspect the prefix-construction script and verify that for each dataset it (a) samples few-shot examples per category from the training split using a normal-distribution-based sampler (fixed seed 42), (b) formats them using the LM-BFF / Gao et al. prompt template, and (c) emits a shared prefix string. Run the constructor and confirm output prefixes contain examples drawn from all class labels and match the documented template structure.

sub_tasks.requirements: Approximately 100 SST-2 prefixes of length ~3.8k tokens are constructed (yielding ~67 GB KV cache for Qwen2.5-7B, ~226 GB for 14B, ~301 GB for 32B).

verification.spec: Run the SST-2 prefix construction pipeline and assert that ~100 prefixes are produced and the mean token length (via Qwen2.5 tokenizer) is ~3.8k. Also compute KV-cache size = 2 * num_layers * num_kv_heads * head_dim * seq_len * bytes_per_elem for Qwen2.5-7B and verify ~67 GB total across 100 prefixes within tolerance.

sub_tasks.requirements: Approximately 110 Subj prefixes of length ~4.4k tokens are constructed (yielding ~67 GB KV cache for Qwen2.5-7B, ~230 GB for 14B, ~305 GB for 32B).

verification.spec: Run the Subj prefix construction pipeline and assert ~110 prefixes are produced with mean token length ~4.4k (Qwen2.5 tokenizer). Verify Qwen2.5-7B KV cache total ~67 GB within tolerance.

sub_tasks.requirements: Approximately 120 TREC prefixes of length ~5k tokens are constructed (yielding ~75 GB KV cache for Qwen2.5-7B, ~257 GB for 14B, ~343 GB for 32B).

verification.spec: Run the TREC prefix construction pipeline and assert ~120 prefixes are produced with mean token length ~5k (Qwen2.5 tokenizer). Verify Qwen2.5-7B KV cache total ~75 GB within tolerance.

sub_tasks.requirements: Approximately 80 RTE prefixes of length ~6k tokens are constructed (yielding ~68 GB KV cache for Qwen2.5-7B, ~233 GB for 14B, ~311 GB for 32B).

verification.spec: Run the RTE prefix construction pipeline and assert ~80 prefixes are produced with mean token length ~6k (Qwen2.5 tokenizer). Verify Qwen2.5-7B KV cache total ~68 GB within tolerance.

sub_tasks.requirements: Pre-computed prefix KV caches for each constructed prefix are generated using a normal prefill pass and persistently stored on the SSD, indexed for reuse across requests.

verification.spec: Run a normal full prefill on each constructed prefix using Qwen2.5-7B, capture each layer's K and V tensors, and persist them as files on the SSD. Verify that (a) one file/index entry exists per prefix, (b) loading the stored KV and concatenating with new suffix KV reproduces the same first-token logits (within fp16 noise) as a from-scratch prefill of prefix+suffix.

sub_tasks.requirements: A fixed random seed of 42 is used in all experiments to ensure reproducibility.

verification.spec: Grep the codebase for seed initialization (torch.manual_seed, np.random.seed, random.seed) and assert the default seed value passed in experiment scripts is 42.

sub_tasks.requirements: The Re-Prefill phase as defined in the paper has been implemented.

The Re-Prefill phase computes K and V for the new, non-shared suffix tokens and performs attention over the concatenation of the loaded prefix KV cache and the new KV cache to generate the first token.

verification.spec: Given a stored prefix KV cache and a new suffix, run the Re-Prefill engine and verify it (a) computes K_suffix, V_suffix via the layer's W_k, W_v projections on the suffix tokens, (b) concatenates [K_prefix, K_suffix] and [V_prefix, V_suffix] for attention with Q_suffix, (c) returns a first-token logit vector matching a reference full-prefill of prefix+suffix within fp16 numerical tolerance.

sub_tasks.requirements: Time-To-First-Token (TTFT) is measured as the wall-clock latency of the Re-Prefill phase (from request start to first generated token).

verification.spec: Verify the benchmarking harness measures TTFT as elapsed wall-clock time (using torch.cuda.Event with synchronization or time.perf_counter with cuda sync) from request submission to the moment the first generated token is produced. Inspect the timer placement in code and run a sanity test where an artificial sleep before first-token generation increases reported TTFT by the same amount.

sub_tasks.requirements: The ContiguousChunk granularity-alignment design (Section 4.2) has been implemented.
Input prefixes of length n are partitioned into m = ceil(n/c) consecutive ContiguousChunks, each containing up to c tokens.

verification.spec: Call the chunking function with a synthetic prefix of length n (e.g., n=100, c=16) and assert the returned number of ContiguousChunks equals ceil(n/c) = 7, with the first 6 chunks holding 16 tokens each and the last chunk holding 4 tokens. Repeat for n=160 (exact divisor) and n=1 (edge case).

sub_tasks.requirements: The default ContiguousChunk size c is set to 16 tokens.

verification.spec: Inspect the default configuration (config file, argparse default, or class default) for ContiguousChunk size (e.g., parameter named chunk_size, semchunk_size, or c) and assert its default value is 16.

sub_tasks.requirements: ContiguousChunk is used as the unified granularity for importance scoring, on-SSD storage layout, host-to-device transfer, and in-memory caching (i.e., the same unit is used for eviction, storage, and prefetching).

verification.spec: Run a small inference and instrument the SSD I/O layer, the cache eviction logic, and the prefetcher. Assert that (a) the unit size of each SSD read equals ContiguousChunk KV size, (b) the eviction granularity (items in the min-heaps) is ContiguousChunk, and (c) prefetch requests are issued in units of ContiguousChunk. All three observed unit sizes must equal c*per_token_kv_bytes for the configured layer.

sub_tasks.requirements: Importance selection of ContiguousChunks for a layer is performed using a chunk-level KV cache pruning algorithm (e.g., ChunkKV / chunk-aggregated H2O scores) where the chunk index set is computed once per Period.

verification.spec: Verify that the importance selection module aggregates token-level scores (e.g., chunk-aggregated H2O / ChunkKV) into per-ContiguousChunk scores and selects top-K chunk indices, and that this index set is computed only on the first layer of a Period and reused for subsequent layers in the same Period (assert chunk-set equality across layers within the Period via instrumentation).

sub_tasks.requirements: SSD storage is laid out so that all K and V values for the tokens of a single ContiguousChunk for a given layer reside contiguously, allowing one I/O request per important ContiguousChunk with no read amplification.

verification.spec: Inspect the on-SSD layout writer and reader: persist KV cache for a synthetic prefix, then for a randomly chosen layer and ContiguousChunk, verify (via file offsets / pread) that all 2*c*head_dim*num_kv_heads bytes for that chunk are stored at contiguous byte offsets. Issue a single read for one important chunk and assert bytes_read == chunk_kv_size_bytes (read amplification factor == 1.0).

sub_tasks.requirements: The reuse-aware asynchronous prefetching engine (Section 4.3) has been implemented.
Consecutive layers are grouped into Periods of size p (default p=8) that share the same set of important ContiguousChunk indices.

verification.spec: Inspect the default configuration for Period size p and assert default is 8. Also verify in InferenceEngine code that, within a Period, the critical ContiguousChunk indices identified at the first layer are reused for all p layers (e.g., via a check that indices are cached per-period and not recomputed per-layer).

sub_tasks.requirements: For each Period, the important ContiguousChunk index set is determined during the first layer of the Period (loading probing keys, computing Q, and identifying critical chunks) and reused for the remaining p-1 layers in that Period.

verification.spec: Instrument the engine to log, for each Period of p layers, the layer index at which the critical ContiguousChunk index set is computed and the index sets used by each of the p layers in the Period. Assert that computation happens only at the Period's first layer (probing-key load + Q-projection + chunk identification), and that layers 2..p reuse the exact same index set.

sub_tasks.requirements: Inter-Period prefetching: before Period i (i>0) begins computation, the system asynchronously prefetches the critical ContiguousChunks of the previous Period (i-1) as a speculative warm-up for Period i.

verification.spec: Instrument the engine and run inference over at least 2 Periods. Assert that before Period i (i>=1) begins computation, prefetch I/O requests are issued for ContiguousChunks corresponding to Period (i-1)'s critical indices, and that these requests target Period i layers. Verify by logging issue-time vs. period-start-time and chunk-index set.

sub_tasks.requirements: Once the first layer of Period i identifies its own critical ContiguousChunk set, only the missing chunks (i.e., the difference between Period i's chunk set and the speculatively prefetched set from Period i-1) are loaded from storage.

verification.spec: Run a Re-Prefill with inter-period prefetching enabled. For each Period i>0, log the prefetched chunk set S_prefetch (taken from Period i-1's indices), the actual chunk set S_i identified by layer 1 of Period i, and the set of chunks issued for synchronous load. Assert that the synchronously loaded set equals S_i \ S_prefetch (the missing difference) and contains no chunks already in S_prefetch.

sub_tasks.requirements: A SubPeriod size sp parameter is implemented that controls how many layers' KV cache must be fully loaded before computation for the current Period can begin (default sp=4).

verification.spec: Inspect the configuration for SubPeriod size (sp) parameter and assert default is 4. Verify in source that the prefetch/compute scheduler uses sp to determine how many layers' KV cache must be loaded before computation for the current Period begins (e.g., a barrier or wait on the first sp layers' loads).

sub_tasks.requirements: Separate CUDA / OS streams are used so that the I/O stream and the compute stream can execute concurrently, enabling the depicted pipelined Re-Prefill execution.

verification.spec: Inspect the engine source and verify dedicated CUDA streams (or asyncio/threaded I/O workers + dedicated copy stream) are created for I/O (SSD->CPU->GPU transfers) and compute (attention/FFN kernels). Run with NVTX/torch profiler and confirm I/O memcpy operations and compute kernels overlap on different streams during Re-Prefill.

sub_tasks.requirements: The attention-guided cache management policy (Section 4.4) has been implemented.
Per-token attention scores are computed using softmax(h_q * h_k) and summed over the query dimension to yield per-token importance a_i.

verification.spec: Unit-test the importance-scoring function: given synthetic h_q, h_k tensors of shape [n, d], verify it computes h_qk = softmax(h_q @ h_k^T / sqrt(d)) (or the form used by the model) and then a_i = sum over query dimension (axis=0) of h_qk, returning a length-n vector. Compare against a NumPy reference implementation.

sub_tasks.requirements: Per-ContiguousChunk attention importance A_j is computed by summing the per-token attention scores a_i over the c tokens in the j-th ContiguousChunk.

verification.spec: Unit-test per-chunk importance aggregation: given a per-token importance vector a of length n and chunk size c, assert that A_j = sum_{i=c*(j-1)}^{c*j - 1} a_i for j=1..ceil(n/c), matching a reference NumPy reshape-and-sum implementation; verify edge-case handling for n not divisible by c.

sub_tasks.requirements: A cumulative attention-based importance I_j is maintained per ContiguousChunk, initialized to 0 and updated as I_j = I_j + A_j every time chunk j participates in a request's computation.

verification.spec: Instantiate the CacheManager, simulate processing of N requests where chunk j has known attention scores A_j^(1), A_j^(2), ..., A_j^(N). After each request, assert that the stored I_j equals the cumulative sum of A_j values. Verify that I_j is initialized to 0 before any request is processed.

sub_tasks.requirements: An access frequency counter F_j is maintained per ContiguousChunk, incremented each time chunk j is loaded into GPU or CPU memory.

verification.spec: Instantiate CacheManager and trigger a sequence of loads of chunk j into GPU memory and CPU memory. After each load event, assert that F_j increments by 1. Verify initial F_j is 0 and that other chunks' counters do not change.

sub_tasks.requirements: The cache score S_j for ContiguousChunk j is computed as the product S_j = I_j * F_j.

verification.spec: Construct a CacheManager with synthetic ContiguousChunks. Manually set I_j and F_j for several chunks, call the cache-score computation, and assert returned S_j == I_j * F_j exactly for each chunk.

sub_tasks.requirements: Two min-heaps are maintained (one for the GPU cache, one for the CPU cache), keyed by cache score S_j, and used to select low-score ContiguousChunks for eviction.

verification.spec: Inspect CacheManager and assert two min-heap data structures exist (one per tier: GPU, CPU), keyed by S_j. Insert N synthetic chunks with random scores, request eviction, and verify the chunk with the smallest S_j is evicted from each heap. Verify chunks evicted from the GPU heap are inserted into the CPU heap when their scores remain above the CPU eviction threshold.

sub_tasks.requirements: ContiguousChunks evicted from the GPU heap are inserted into the CPU heap if their scores remain relatively high; otherwise they are evicted from memory entirely and remain only on SSD.

verification.spec: Fill the GPU min-heap to capacity, then insert a chunk with high score forcing eviction of a low-score chunk. Verify that when evicted from GPU, the chunk is inserted into the CPU heap if its score is above the CPU heap's min, otherwise removed entirely from memory while remaining accessible via SSD. Test both paths by varying scores.

sub_tasks.requirements: An in-memory table stores cache scores S_j for all ContiguousChunks, including those that are no longer in CPU or GPU memory.

verification.spec: Inspect CacheManager: verify it maintains an in-memory dict/table mapping chunk_id -> S_j for ALL chunks ever seen. Evict a chunk from CPU and GPU memory and assert its score remains retrievable from the table.

sub_tasks.requirements: Implementation details and memory budgeting from Section 4.5 have been respected.
The InferenceEngine class implements granularity-aligned ContiguousChunk handling and the two-level (intra/inter-period) prefetching mechanism.

verification.spec: Import InferenceEngine class; verify it exposes methods for ContiguousChunk-based KV loading, intra-period prefetching, and inter-period prefetching. Check via importlib and inspect that these methods exist and are invoked during a forward pass.

sub_tasks.requirements: The CacheManager class implements the attention-guided cache management policy with GPU/CPU min-heaps.

verification.spec: Import CacheManager class via importlib; verify it exposes GPU and CPU min-heap structures and an attention-guided eviction policy method. Confirm methods for inserting/evicting based on cache score.

sub_tasks.requirements: Memory budgets are partitioned so that under a 10 GB total memory constraint, exactly 0.2 GB of GPU memory and 0.4 GB of CPU memory are reserved as prefetch buffers (with the remainder used as the actual cache).

verification.spec: Inspect the CacheManager / InferenceEngine code and assert that with a 10 GB total memory budget the GPU prefetch buffer is allocated 0.2 GB and the CPU prefetch buffer is allocated 0.4 GB, with the remainder reserved as cache. Verify by instantiating the manager with budget=10GB and reading buffer/cache size attributes.

sub_tasks.requirements: Prefetched data is held only in the dedicated prefetch buffer and does not consume additional memory beyond the configured budget.

verification.spec: Configure 10GB total memory budget with 0.2GB GPU prefetch buffer and 0.4GB CPU prefetch buffer. Monitor allocated memory via torch.cuda.memory_allocated() and psutil during Re-Prefill. Assert peak memory does not exceed the configured budget.

sub_tasks.requirements: Baseline offloading-based inference systems used for comparison have been reimplemented.

The AS+LRU baseline (AttentionStore-style system) has been reimplemented: it serves LLM inference with full prefix KV caches over a multi-tier storage system using LRU as the cache policy, with scheduler-aware optimizations disabled.

verification.spec: Run the AS+LRU baseline on a short prefix with two requests. Assert that (a) the full prefix KV cache is loaded (no token pruning is applied), (b) the cache eviction policy used is LRU (verify via inspecting the cache class or behavior: evict the least-recently-used chunk), and (c) scheduler-aware optimizations are disabled (no scheduler hooks invoked).

sub_tasks.requirements: The AS+H2O+LRU baseline has been reimplemented: AS+LRU augmented with the H2O important-token selection algorithm so that only important tokens (not full prefixes) are loaded into memory.

verification.spec: Run AS+H2O+LRU baseline on a short prefix. Assert (a) H2O important-token selection is applied (only tokens flagged by H2O are loaded into memory; the number loaded equals budget_ratio * prefix_length within rounding), (b) LRU eviction policy governs the in-memory cache, and (c) it builds on top of the AS+LRU multi-tier storage system.

sub_tasks.requirements: The chunk size used by AS+LRU, AS+H2O+LRU, and IMPRESS is set to 64 tokens.

verification.spec: Inspect baseline configurations for AS+LRU, AS+H2O+LRU, and IMPRESS implementations and assert the chunk_size parameter for each is set to 64 by default.

sub_tasks.requirements: Evaluation infrastructure, metrics, and hardware configuration are correctly set up.
Experiments are run on a server with 2x Intel Xeon Platinum 8370C CPUs (64 cores), 128 GB DRAM, one NVIDIA A800 GPU with 80 GB HBM2e, and a 4 TB Samsung 990 Pro NVMe SSD connected via PCIe 4.0 x16.

verification.spec: Run a hardware probe script: query nvidia-smi for GPU model (expect 'A800' with 80GB), query /proc/cpuinfo for 'Xeon Platinum 8370C' (or note CPU model used), check total DRAM via /proc/meminfo (~128GB), and probe the NVMe device via nvme list or lsblk for a Samsung 990 Pro 4TB. Run a quick fio sequential read benchmark on the SSD and assert peak read bandwidth >= 6 GB/s (paper measured 7.45 GB/s).

sub_tasks.requirements: Measured SSD peak read bandwidth (~7.45 GB/s) and PCIe bidirectional bandwidth (~32 GB/s) are verified and documented in the experimental setup.

verification.spec: Run an SSD sequential read benchmark (e.g., fio with bs=1M, iodepth=32, sequential read) and assert measured peak read bandwidth around 7.45 GB/s. Run a GPU<->CPU memcpy benchmark (e.g., cudaMemcpyAsync) and assert measured PCIe bandwidth around 32 GB/s.

sub_tasks.requirements: Available GPU memory is fixed at 10 GB and CPU memory at 24 GB for all baselines and for ContiguousKV.

verification.spec: Inspect the experiment configuration / launch scripts and assert that GPU memory budget is set to 10 GB and CPU memory budget is set to 24 GB for ContiguousKV and all baselines (AS+LRU, AS+H2O+LRU, IMPRESS).

sub_tasks.requirements: Accuracy is computed in a prompt-based few-shot manner following LM-BFF / ReFusion conventions (single-token label generation).

verification.spec: Inspect the evaluation script: verify it uses few-shot prompt templates consistent with LM-BFF, generates only a single token (or short label), maps it to a label word, and computes accuracy as the fraction matching ground truth. Run on a small subset and confirm output is a single label token.

sub_tasks.requirements: Average TTFT is measured per system, dataset, and budget ratio, with both GPU and CPU caches warmed up before measurement.

verification.spec: Run end-to-end benchmark harness: verify a warmup phase populates both GPU and CPU caches before timing begins, then per-request TTFT is recorded and averaged. Inspect the script to confirm warmup is non-empty and timing excludes warmup.

sub_tasks.requirements: P95 tail latency of TTFT is measured per system, dataset, and budget ratio.

verification.spec: Run the benchmark harness; verify the output reports the 95th percentile TTFT per (system, dataset, budget) configuration. Inspect log/CSV to confirm P95 is computed via numpy.percentile or equivalent over the collected per-request TTFTs.

sub_tasks.requirements: Experiments are run at KV cache budget ratios of 5%, 10%, 25%, and 50% for all systems.

verification.spec: Inspect experiment scripts/configs and assert that the KV cache budget ratio parameter accepts and is exercised at values {0.05, 0.10, 0.25, 0.50} for all systems.

sub_tasks.requirements: A read amplification metric is implemented that measures (bytes read from SSD) / (bytes of important KV data actually consumed by attention), at the granularity of chunks.

verification.spec: Construct a synthetic scenario with known important-token bytes (B_useful) inside known chunks (B_loaded). Invoke the loader and assert the computed metric equals B_loaded / B_useful within float tolerance. Test multiple cases: full chunk needed (=1), single token in chunk (high amplification).

sub_tasks.requirements: Motivation experiments from Section 3 have been reproduced.
The IMPRESS read amplification histogram is reproduced on Qwen2.5-7B with RTE at 25% KV cache budget ratio.

verification.spec: Run the reimplemented IMPRESS baseline serving Qwen2.5-7B on the RTE dataset with 25% KV cache budget. For each request, log (a) total bytes/tokens loaded from SSD via chunk-based offloading and (b) the number of tokens actually required by the sparse KV cache pruning algorithm (H2O-style). Compute per-request amplification = loaded_tokens / required_tokens. Build a histogram matching Figure 4 and verify the distribution peaks around amplification factor ~12 (i.e., median or modal amplification within [10, 15]). Report the median amplification value and compare to the paper's reported ~12x with tolerance 0.3 (i.e., between 8.4 and 15.6).

sub_tasks.requirements: About 31% of requests exhibit ~12x read amplification under IMPRESS in this setting.

verification.spec: Reproduce IMPRESS serving Qwen2.5-7B on the RTE dataset with a 25% KV cache budget ratio. For each served request, compute the read amplification factor as (bytes loaded from SSD) / (bytes of important KV tokens actually used). Bin the per-request amplification factors into the histogram bins shown in Figure 4 (12-17, 17-22, 22-27, ...). Report the fraction of requests falling in the modal bin (12-17, which corresponds to ~12x read amplification). Assert that this fraction is approximately 31% (i.e., within tolerance of 0.1, so between 21% and 41%).

sub_tasks.requirements: The IMPRESS latency breakdown experiment is reproduced for KV budget ratios 5%, 10%, 25%, and 50%, decomposing each Re-Prefill into QKV Projection, Loading Probing Keys, Identifying Critical Tokens, Loading Critical KVs, and Attention/FFN stages.

verification.spec: Run IMPRESS Re-Prefill on Qwen2.5-7B + RTE at KV budgets 5%, 10%, 25%, 50%. Use CUDA events / timers around each of the 5 stages (QKV projection, load probing keys, identify critical tokens, load critical KVs, attention/FFN). Verify the breakdown CSV is produced and the stages sum to total Re-Prefill latency.

sub_tasks.requirements: I/O-intensive stages (Loading Probing Keys + Loading Critical KVs) account for more than 65% of total IMPRESS Re-Prefill time across all four budget ratios.

verification.spec: From the reproduced latency breakdown at all four budget ratios, compute (loading_probing_keys + loading_critical_KVs) / total. Assert this ratio exceeds 0.65 at each of 5%, 10%, 25%, and 50%.

sub_tasks.requirements: The cross-layer similarity experiment of important ContiguousChunk indices is reproduced on Qwen2.5-7B (RTE and SST-2 at 5% and 25% budgets), showing high Jaccard similarity between adjacent layers and degradation for distant layers.

verification.spec: Run Qwen2.5-7B on RTE and SST-2 at 5% and 25% budgets. For each request, record the set of important ContiguousChunk indices per layer. Compute Jaccard similarity between every layer pair and produce a heatmap. Verify adjacent-layer similarity is high (>0.7) and diagonal-distant similarity decreases, matching Figure 7(a).

sub_tasks.requirements: The cross-Period coverage ratio experiment is reproduced, showing inter-Period similarity of important ContiguousChunk indices in the 52%-64% range on average.

verification.spec: Group Qwen2.5-7B's 28 layers into Periods (p=4 or 8), compute important ContiguousChunk index set per Period (from the first layer of each Period), then compute coverage |I_i ∩ I_{i+1}| / |I_{i+1}| between adjacent Periods, averaged across requests on RTE/SST-2 at 5% and 25% budgets. Assert coverage falls in 0.52-0.64 range.

sub_tasks.requirements: End-to-end accuracy results (Figure 9) have been reproduced.
Accuracy is measured for ContiguousKV, AS+LRU, AS+H2O+LRU, and IMPRESS on SST-2 with Qwen2.5-7B/14B/32B at KV budget ratios of 5%, 10%, 25%, and 50%.

verification.spec: Run the 4 systems on SST-2 with Qwen2.5-7B, 14B, and 32B at budget ratios 5%, 10%, 25%, 50% (48 configurations). Verify output CSV reports accuracy for each cell and that ContiguousKV results approximately match Figure 9(a).

sub_tasks.requirements: Accuracy is measured for ContiguousKV, AS+LRU, AS+H2O+LRU, and IMPRESS on Subj with Qwen2.5-7B/14B/32B at KV budget ratios of 5%, 10%, 25%, and 50%.

verification.spec: Run the 4 systems on Subj with Qwen2.5-7B, 14B, and 32B at budget ratios 5%, 10%, 25%, 50%. Verify output CSV reports accuracy for each cell matching Figure 9(b).

sub_tasks.requirements: Accuracy is measured for ContiguousKV, AS+LRU, AS+H2O+LRU, and IMPRESS on TREC with Qwen2.5-7B/14B/32B at KV budget ratios of 5%, 10%, 25%, and 50%.

verification.spec: Run the 4 systems on TREC with Qwen2.5-7B, 14B, and 32B at budget ratios 5%, 10%, 25%, 50%. Verify output CSV reports accuracy matching Figure 9(c).

sub_tasks.requirements: Accuracy is measured for ContiguousKV, AS+LRU, AS+H2O+LRU, and IMPRESS on RTE with Qwen2.5-7B/14B/32B at KV budget ratios of 5%, 10%, 25%, and 50%.

verification.spec: Run the 4 systems on RTE with Qwen2.5-7B, 14B, and 32B at budget ratios 5%, 10%, 25%, 50%. Verify output CSV reports accuracy matching Figure 9(d).

sub_tasks.requirements: AS+LRU accuracy does not change across budget ratios because it loads the full KV cache.

verification.spec: Run ContiguousKV Re-Prefill end-to-end on RTE (or SST-2) with Qwen2.5-7B varying sp in {0,1,2,4,8}. Record avg TTFT. Assert sp=4 TTFT is the minimum (or within 10% of minimum) and that sp=8 TTFT > sp=4 TTFT (intra-period pipelining disabled at sp==Period). Report TTFT values.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that ContiguousKV achieves ~2.6x speedup over IMPRESS at a 10K-token shared prefix on Qwen2.5-7B with 25% KV cache budget, and the speedup ratio grows with prefix length

verification.spec: Sweep shared-prefix lengths in {2K, 4K, 6K, 10K} tokens with Qwen2.5-7B at 25% KV budget. Measure avg TTFT for both ContiguousKV and IMPRESS baseline. Assert TTFT_IMPRESS / TTFT_ContiguousKV at 10K tokens >= 2.6 within tolerance, and that this ratio increases monotonically (non-strict) with prefix length.

sub_tasks.requirements: Hardware / library platforms used in the paper
Code has been implemented and run such that the GPU-host interconnect on the experimental machine is PCIe 4.0 x16 and the measured bidirectional bandwidth between host DRAM and the GPU is ~32 GB/s

verification.spec: Run a pinned-memory cudaMemcpyAsync benchmark (H2D + D2H concurrent) transferring >=1 GB and measure achieved aggregate bandwidth. Assert |measured - 32| / 32 <= tolerance. Also assert nvidia-smi reports PCIe Gen4 x16.

sub_tasks.requirements: Method components (from critique splits and additions)

Code has been implemented such that the IMPRESS baseline performs a partial 'probing key' load per layer (only key vectors, not full KV) to support importance identification before the critical-KV load

verification.spec: Instantiate the IMPRESS baseline and run one Re-Prefill layer with a hook that records SSD reads. Assert that two distinct read phases are issued per layer: (1) a 'probing keys' phase that loads only K-tensors (no V-tensors) with size matching the chunked key budget, and (2) a subsequent 'critical KV' phase that loads both K and V for the selected chunks. Assert phase (1) byte count == expected key-only chunked-prefix size.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that the IMPRESS baseline manages KV data in fixed 64-token chunks of approximately 1.8 MB each, with SSD I/O aligned to chunk boundaries

verification.spec: Inspect the IMPRESS baseline configuration: assert chunk_size == 64 tokens. For Qwen2.5-7B (hidden=3584, num_kv_heads=4, fp16), compute expected chunk bytes = 64 * (3584/28*4) * 2 * 2 ≈ 1.8 MB per layer-chunk, and assert that each SSD read issued by IMPRESS is a multiple of the per-layer chunk size.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that the IMPRESS baseline uses the score-based cache policy from the IMPRESS paper (frequency-weighted importance), not plain LRU or LFU

verification.spec: Construct a synthetic sequence of chunk accesses where LRU, LFU, and the IMPRESS score policy each yield a different eviction order. Run the IMPRESS cache manager and assert its eviction order matches the IMPRESS score policy and differs from both LRU and LFU.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that the IMPRESS baseline overlaps the prefix-key prefetch of layer L+1 with the attention/FFN compute of layer L (limited single-layer-ahead prefetching)

verification.spec: Run the IMPRESS baseline through 2 layers with CUDA stream and I/O event tracing. Assert that the I/O event 'load_probing_keys(L+1)' starts before the compute event 'attention(L)' completes, but that 'load_critical_KVs(L+1)' does NOT start until after 'identify_critical(L+1)' (preserving the sequential dependency).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that in ContiguousKV, immediately after critical-index identification at the first layer L1 of a Period, a single batched async I/O is issued for the critical ContiguousChunks of all p layers in the Period

verification.spec: Trace the I/O stream during one Period (p=8) of ContiguousKV. Assert that within a window of <5 ms after identify_critical(L1) returns, async load requests are issued covering KV chunks for layers L1..L8 (all p layers of the Period), and no additional identify_critical call is made for L2..L8.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that within a Period, per-layer KV cache loads execute on the I/O CUDA stream concurrently with the previous layer's attention/FFN compute on the compute stream

verification.spec: Profile one Period of ContiguousKV with CUDA events on the compute stream and the I/O stream. For each adjacent layer pair (Li, Li+1) within the Period, assert that the start time of 'load_KV(Li+1)' on the I/O stream is < the end time of 'attention(Li)'+'ffn(Li)' on the compute stream (genuine overlap).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that for layer L_l (l>1) within a Period, ContiguousKV begins the attention kernel without issuing a blocking synchronous load (the KV data has already been prefetched into GPU memory)

verification.spec: Instrument ContiguousKV to log any synchronous .wait()/cudaStreamSynchronize() calls on the I/O stream immediately before attention launch. Run a Period of p=8 layers with a warm cache and assert that for layers L2..L8 no blocking wait of >1 ms occurs before attention launch (only L1 may block).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that at the start of Re-Prefill, the full prefix KV cache resides on the slower storage tier (CPU memory and/or SSD), not pre-loaded on the GPU

verification.spec: Before invoking Re-Prefill for a fresh request with a cold GPU cache, query the cache manager state. Assert that GPU-resident KV chunk count for the shared prefix is 0 (or <= prefetch buffer size of 0.2 GB), and that the prefix KV cache total bytes are accounted for on CPU and/or SSD tiers.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that for every layer L_l, the prefix K and V tensors required for that layer are present in GPU memory before the attention kernel is launched (enforcing the sequential dependency correctly)

verification.spec: Add a pre-attention assertion hook that, for each layer, checks the GPU-resident set of prefix KV ContiguousChunks contains every chunk index in the critical-index set. Run Re-Prefill for at least 5 requests across all layers and assert the hook never fires a missing-chunk error.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that ContiguousKV's Re-Prefill output (first-token logits) on Qwen2.5-7B with budget=100% (no pruning) matches a from-scratch full prefill on the concatenated prefix+suffix within fp16 numerical tolerance

verification.spec: For 5 sample (prefix, suffix) pairs from RTE, (a) run vanilla HF Qwen2.5-7B prefill on prefix+suffix and capture first-token logits; (b) precompute prefix KV via ContiguousKV's offline path, then run ContiguousKV Re-Prefill on the same suffix with budget_ratio=1.0 (no pruning). Assert max|logits_a - logits_b| / max|logits_a| <= tolerance and that argmax tokens match.
